2604.03630.title: A Multimodal Foundation Model of Spatial Transcriptomics and Histology for Biological Discovery and Clinical Prediction
2604.03630.paper_summary: Spatial transcriptomics (ST) enables gene expression mapping within anatomical context but remains costly and low-throughput. Hematoxylin and eosin (H\&E) staining offers rich morphology yet lacks molecular resolution. We present \textbf{\ours} (\textbf{S}patial \textbf{T}ranscriptomics and hist\textbf{O}logy \textbf{R}epresentation \textbf{M}odel), a foundation model trained on 1.2 million spatially resolved transcriptomic profiles with matched histology across 18 organs. Using a hierarchical architecture integrating morphological features, gene expression, and spatial context, STORM bridges imaging and omics through robust molecular--morphological representations. STORM enhances spatial domain discovery, producing biologically coherent tissue maps, and outperforms existing methods in predicting spatial gene expression from H\&E images across 11 tumor types. The model is platform-agnostic, performing consistently across Visium, Xenium, Visium HD, and CosMx. Applied to 23 independent cohorts comprising 7,245 patients, STORM significantly improves immunotherapy response prediction and prognostication over established biomarkers, providing a scalable framework for spatially informed discovery and clinical precision medicine.
2604.03630.paper_summary_zh: 空間轉錄組學（ST）能夠在解剖學背景下進行基因表達映射，但仍然成本高且通量低。蘇木精-伊紅（H\&E）染色提供了豐富的形態學特徵，但缺乏分子解析度。我們提出了\textbf{\ours}（\textbf{S}patial \textbf{T}ranscriptomics and hist\textbf{O}logy \textbf{R}epresentation \textbf{M}odel），這是一個基於120萬個具有空間解析的轉錄組特徵和18個器官的配對組織學的基礎模型。利用整合形態特徵、基因表達和空間背景的分層架構，STORM通過穩健的分子-形態表示橋接了成像和組學。STORM增強了空間領域的發現，生成生物學上連貫的組織圖，並在預測11種腫瘤類型的H\&E影像中的空間基因表達方面超越了現有方法。該模型是平台無關的，在Visium、Xenium、Visium HD和CosMx上表現一致。應用於23個獨立的隊列，包括7,245名患者，STORM顯著改善了免疫療法反應預測和預後，超越了既有的生物標記，提供了一個可擴展的框架，用於基於空間的發現和臨床精準醫療。
2604.03630.author: Jinxi Xiang et.al.
2604.03630.authors: Jinxi Xiang, Siyu Hou, Yuchen Li, Ryan Quinton, Xiaoming Zhang, Feyisope Eweje, Xiangde Luo, Yijiang Chen, Zhe Li, Colin Bergstrom, Ted Kim, Sierra Willens, Francesca Maria Olguin, Matthew Abikenari, Andrew Heider, Sanjeeth Rajaram, Joel Neal, Maximilian Diehn, Xiang Zhou, Ruijiang Li
